content.problemStatement:
You are given a **randomly generated** permutation PPP of length NNN. You can perform the following operation any number of times (including zero):


Choose an index iii such that 1≤i<N1 ≤ i < N1≤i<N, and update:

Pi=min⁡(Pi,Pi+1)andPi+1=min⁡(Pi,Pi+1)\begin{aligned}  
P_i &= \min(P_i, P_{i+1}) \quad and &  
P_{i+1} &= \min(P_i, P_{i+1})  
\end{aligned}  Pi​​=min(Pi​,Pi+1​)and​Pi+1​​=min(Pi​,Pi+1​)​
In other words, replace both PiP_iPi​ and Pi+1P_{i+1}Pi+1​ with the smaller of the two values.


An array AAA of length NNN is called reachable if it is possible to obtain AAA from the original permutation PPP  by applying the operation described above a finite number of times.

Your task is to determine the number of distinct reachable arrays that can be obtained from PPP.

**Note:**


A permutation of length NNN is an array A=[A1,A2,…,AN]A = [A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_N]A=[A1​,A2​,…,AN​] such that every integer from 111 to NNN occurs exactly once in AAA. For example, [1,2,3][1, 2, 3][1,2,3] and [2,3,1][2, 3, 1][2,3,1] are permutations of length 333, but [1,2,1][1, 2, 1][1,2,1], [4,1,2][4, 1, 2][4,1,2], and [2,3,1,4][2, 3, 1, 4][2,3,1,4] are not.
A randomly generated permutation is a permutation that is chosen randomly from the N!N!N! possible permutations with equal probability.

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN — the length of permutation.
The next line contains NNN space separated integers P1,P2,...,PNP_1,P_2,...,P_NP1​,P2​,...,PN​, denoting the elements of the permutation.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of distinct reachable arrays, modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1: Some of the possible reachable arrays are: [1,1,1,1],[4,1,1,1],[4,3,1,1],[4,2,2,1][1, 1, 1, 1], [4, 1, 1, 1], [4, 3, 1, 1], [4, 2 , 2, 1][1,1,1,1],[4,1,1,1],[4,3,1,1],[4,2,2,1]. There are 14 such reachable arrays possible.
